dialogue.utterance: Hi, I'm Jordan, an EMT with the rescue squad. I see [CITY] Fire is already on scene. What's the situation as you understand it?
Dispatch to Unit 3 responding for cardiac arrest.
His family says he has diabetes and memory issues. He was found on the floor not breathing and not responding to anyone.
Let me assess his responsiveness. No eye opening, verbal, or motor response.
No eye opening at all to any stimulus. GCS-E1.
No verbal response to voice, pain, or shaking. GCS-V1.
I tried applying sternum rub and loud voice. No motor response. GCS-M1.
GCS is 3. No movement after vocalization or pain stimulus.
Airway is patent and in open position. No signs of obstruction or foreign body.
No breathing. [CITY] is using BVM-no bilateral breath sounds. Asymmetric chest movement.
Chest remains still-no rise and fall. Audible sounds absent. Capnography zero.
The skin is cool and dry. No obvious bleeding or trauma. Peripheral pulses zero.
Vital signs-blood pressure is unmeasurable. No central pulse.
Bystander, can you confirm any medical history from his chart?
His chart lists a history of Alzheimer's disease and type 1 diabetes.
Any known medications or allergies?
No known drug allergies. He uses insulin, but the family is unsure of exact dosage or frequency.
Last time he was seen normal?
His niece saw him at 12:10 PM in full cognition. He was found unresponsive at 12:40 PM.
Was there any history of chest pain before this incident?
No chest pain reported. He was found unresponsive on the floor, not breathing.
What is his name and age?
Not sure of his name, but he is 70 years old, male.
Applying oxygen saturation monitor. EtCO2 reading is zero. Oxygen saturation is at 0%.
Central pulse is undetectable. Peripheral radial and femoral pulses are also zero.
Capnography reading at zero. Respirations are not noted-no chest movement.
Glucose level is 451 mg/dL via fingerstick. No sign of hypoglycemia.
Placing 4-lead ECG. Rhythm is asystole-no complexes or pulses.
Initiating IV line via left antecubital as per protocol.
IV attempt unsuccessful. No vein access-proceeding to intraosseous access.
IO placed in left humerus-confirmed by resistance and flash.
Delivering 1 mg Epinephrine 1:10,000 via IO. First round of medication complete.
Rechecking ECG now. Rhythm is still asystole.
Declaring this a full code and following protocol 2-7 for cardiac arrest resuscitation.
Inserting King airway. Confirming placement via end-tidal CO2 and consistent resistance.
BVM being used continuously with secured airway in place.
Second dose of Epinephrine again delivered via IO. Rhythm remains unchanged.
Administering third Epinephrine dose. Still no return of spontaneous circulation.
EKG still shows no activity. Autopulse in use, no improvement reported.
Time is now 1:12 PM. Still on third dose of Epi. Autopulse pauses every 2 minutes for pulse checks.
GCS still at 3, no eye, verbal, or motor responses to any stimulus.
Family has confirmed decision to stop resuscitation at 1:14 PM today.
Patient is secured on board the stretcher. No indication of changes en route.
No improvement-central and peripheral pulses still at 0.
Continuous capnography is zero. No return of spontaneous breathing.
No further medications or procedures needed. Family decided against continued resuscitation.
No new swelling, bleeding, or trauma has developed since last check.
End-tidal CO2 remains zero. Airway secure and functioning with BVM.
Autopulse pauses approximately every 2 minutes with no improvement.
Rhythm remains asystole with no return of spontaneous circulation-1:14 PM.
No signs of pain or discomfort. Patient is fully non-responsive.
No verbalization or movement indicates pain. Skin condition is unchanged.
Full neurological assessment remains unchanged. GCS-E1V1M1 persisting.
Removing BVM and airway equipment as per family decision.
No rashes, lesions, or new bruising on the skin. Dry and intact.
No edema or discoloration noted. Skin turgor not assessed due to full code.
No signs of moisture or pressure injury developing.
No wounds, abrasions, or other surface injuries detected.
No trauma to the head or neck. No signs of bleeding.
Pupils equal and 4 mm but non-reactive to light.
No abnormalities behind the ears. No nuchal rigidity reported.
No drainage noted from nose or mouth. Oral cavity free of trauma.
No signs of difficulty swallowing or airway swelling.
No breath sounds left or right. Continuing assisted ventilation.
Chest rises evenly with BVM efforts. No asymmetry present.
Abdomen flat and no distension seen. No signs of tenderness.
Bowel sounds have not been heard during assessment.
No blood in urine or signs of renal pain reported.
Limbs in normal alignment. No angulation or unusual swelling.
No signs of lower or upper extremity injuries. Back dry and intact.
Everything remains unchanged post-last intervention.
Ending all life-sustaining efforts as per family and physician orders.
Full code status verified. No advance directive available or indicated.
No ROSC achieved after 31 minutes of ongoing interventions.
Patient being transported to receiving ER as per standing protocol.
